# Template retrieved from a map populated by ParseFiles with non-static paths.
# The tool traces through loadTemplates helper, resolves glob patterns, and
# type-checks templates against the concrete Page type passed at call sites.

check-templates
! stderr .

-- go.mod --
module example.com/app

go 1.25.0
-- main.go --
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"net/http"
	"path/filepath"
)

type Page struct {
	Title   string
	Content string
}

func loadTemplates(root string) map[string]*template.Template {
	layoutFile := filepath.Join(root, "layouts", "base.html")
	partialFiles, _ := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(root, "partials", "*.html"))
	shared := append([]string{layoutFile}, partialFiles...)
	pageFiles, _ := filepath.Glob(filepath.Join(root, "pages", "*", "*.html"))
	templates := make(map[string]*template.Template)
	for _, page := range pageFiles {
		files := make([]string, len(shared)+1)
		copy(files, shared)
		files[len(shared)] = page
		t, err := template.ParseFiles(files...)
		if err != nil {
			panic(err)
		}
		name := filepath.Base(page)
		templates[name] = t
	}
	return templates
}

func main() {
	templates := loadTemplates("templates")

	render := func(w http.ResponseWriter, name string, data any) {
		t, ok := templates[name]
		if !ok {
			http.Error(w, "not found", 500)
			return
		}
		_ = t.ExecuteTemplate(w, "base.html", data)
	}

	http.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		render(w, "index.html", Page{Title: "Home", Content: "Welcome"})
	})
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint

-- templates/layouts/base.html --
<h1>{{.Title}}</h1>
<p>{{.Content}}</p>

-- templates/partials/footer.html --
<footer>footer</footer>

-- templates/pages/home/index.html --
{{template "base.html" .}}
